description_long: This object is a large, ground-mounted solar panel array designed for urban energy generation. The structure consists of a grid of dark photovoltaic panels, each with a visible grid pattern, supported by a series of slender metal posts and a lightweight frame. The array is elevated above the ground, allowing for airflow and maintenance access beneath. The panels are arranged in a single, continuous row, forming a rectangular surface that is slightly tilted to optimize sunlight capture. The supporting structure appears to be made of metal, likely aluminum or steel, providing both stability and corrosion resistance. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present in the images, and the array is intended for fixed installation in open urban spaces such as parking lots, parks, or rooftops.
description: A large, ground-mounted solar panel array with a grid of dark photovoltaic panels supported by metal posts and frame, forming a single, slightly tilted rectangular surface. Designed for urban solar energy generation in open spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular grid of solar panels and the supporting posts beneath.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the tilt of the panel array and the depth of the supporting frame.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the length of the array and the alignment of the supporting posts.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1, showing the tilt and structure from the other end.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 15 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metal posts and frame, likely aluminum or steel
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 12 panels
